pages_context.page_text:
THIS LEASE AGREEMENT (the "Lease") is made and entered into this 11th day of March, 
2026, by and between Downstate at LICH Holding Company, Inc., a New York corporation 
("Landlord"), and FedEx Ground Package System, Inc., a Delaware corporation, having its 
principal place of business at 1000 FedEx Drive, Moon Township, PA 15108 ("Tenant").

ARTICLE 1: LEASED PREMISES AND FACILITY SCOPE

1.01 The Property: Landlord hereby leases to Tenant, and Tenant hereby leases from Landlord, 
the real property and four-story shell building located at 74 Amity Street, Brooklyn, NY 11201 
(Block 295, Lot 7). 1.02 Square Footage and Layout: The parties stipulate that the Premises 
comprises approximately 5,431 square feet of gross building area. Tenant intends to utilize the 
ground floor for vehicle "load-and-go" operations and the upper three floors for automated 
"honeycomb" parcel sorting and employee administrative zones. 1.03 Historic District Status: 
Tenant acknowledges the building's location within the Cobble Hill Historic District. Tenant 
agrees that any modifications to the 113-year-old facade must be pre-approved by the NYC 
Landmarks Preservation Commission (LPC) and must align with the "1900-era industrial 
aesthetic" noted in the 2013 appraisal.

ARTICLE 2: TERM AND RENEWAL RIGHTS

2.01 Primary Term: The term of this Lease shall be for twenty (20) years (the "Initial Term"), 
beginning on June 1, 2026 (the "Commencement Date"). 2.02 Extended Terms: Tenant shall 
have three (3) successive options to extend the term for five (5) years each. To exercise an 
option, Tenant must provide written notice to Landlord at least 270 days prior to the expiration 
of the current term. 2.03 Early Access: Landlord shall grant Tenant "Early Possession" on April 
1, 2026, for the sole purpose of installing the FedEx proprietary automated sorting system 
and high-speed data architecture. No Base Rent shall be due during this 60-day fit-out period.

ARTICLE 3: RENTAL PAYMENTS (TRIPLE NET)

3.01 Base Rent Schedule: Tenant shall pay Base Rent in monthly installments as follows:

• 
Years 1-5: $290,000.00 per annum ($24,166.67/mo).

• 
Years 6-10: $333,500.00 per annum ($27,791.67/mo).

• 
Years 11-15: $383,525.00 per annum ($31,960.41/mo).

• 
Years 16-20: $441,053.75 per annum ($36,754.48/mo). 3.02 Additional Rent: As a 
"Triple Net" (NNN) lease, Tenant is responsible for 100% of the Real Estate Taxes (est. 
$28,500/year base), Insurance Premiums, and Common Area Maintenance (CAM), 
despite the building being a single-tenant structure. 3.03 Security Deposit: FedEx shall 
provide a Letter of Credit in the amount of $150,000.00 in lieu of a cash security 
deposit, issued by a major financial institution.

pages_context.page_heading: COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT (FEDEX GROUND EDITION - PART
pages_context.page_text:
ARTICLE 4: TAXES AND ASSESSMENTS

4.01 Payment Responsibility: Tenant shall pay all real property taxes, including "Business 
Improvement District" (BID) assessments, directly to the NYC Department of Finance. 4.02 Tax 
Contests: Tenant shall have the right to contest the validity or amount of any tax assessment at 
its own expense. Landlord agrees to cooperate with Tenant in any such "Certiorari" proceeding, 
provided Landlord incurs no cost.

This second section of the FedEx Ground lease focuses on the unique operational model of 
FedEx Ground, specifically its reliance on independent service providers (ISPs) and the heavy-
duty mechanical requirements for automated sorting.

COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT (FEDEX GROUND EDITION - PART 
2 OF 10)

ARTICLE 5: USE AND INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR OPERATIONS 5.01 Permitted 
Use: The Premises shall be used for a FedEx Ground automated sorting facility, small-
package distribution, and "last-mile" logistics. 5.02 Independent Service Providers (ISPs): 
Landlord acknowledges that FedEx Ground operates via a contract model. FedEx shall have the 
right to permit its Independent Service Providers and their employees to access the Premises 
24/7 for the purposes of vehicle loading, package pickup, and dispatch. 5.03 Compliance with 
"FedEx Safe" Standards: Tenant shall ensure all ISPs adhering to the FedEx brand standards 
also comply with the safety and noise regulations of 74 Amity Street as outlined in this Lease.

ARTICLE 6: MAINTENANCE AND REPAIRS (ABSOLUTE NET) 6.01 Structural 
Integrity: Notwithstanding the "shell" condition of the building at the Commencement Date, 
Tenant assumes all responsibility for the structural maintenance of the four-story brick edifice. 
This includes the stabilization of the foundation and the maintenance of the load-bearing 
masonry walls. 6.02 Roof and Envelope: Tenant shall, at its sole cost, replace the existing roof 
membrane with a high-durability FM Global-rated TPO system within the first twenty-four 
(24) months of the Term. 6.03 Landlord’s Exemption: Landlord shall not be required to make 
any repairs or alterations of any kind upon the Premises during the Term of this Lease.

ARTICLE 7: ALTERATIONS AND "PURPLE TAIL" INFRASTRUCTURE 7.01 The 
FedEx Build-Out: Landlord grants Tenant the right to perform a comprehensive industrial 
retrofit. This "FedEx Build-Out" shall include:

• 
The Sorting Core: Installation of a multi-tier automated conveyor system (the "Sortation 
Spine") spanning floors 1 through 3.

• 
Mezzanine Construction: Installation of high-strength steel mezzanines to maximize 
cubic sorting volume within the 113-year-old shell.

• 
Heavy Power: Upgrading the electrical service to 1,600 Amps to support the automated 
sorters and high-speed "Dimensioners." 7.02 Floor Loading Requirements: Tenant 
shall reinforce the second and third floors to support a minimum live load of 175 lbs/sq. 
ft. to accommodate the weight of the conveyor motors and densely packed parcel 
"honeycombs." 7.03 Landmarks Approval: As the building is a "Four-Story Shell" in a

pages_context.page_heading: COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT (FEDEX GROUND EDITION - PART
pages_context.page_text:
historic district, Tenant must ensure all structural reinforcements are internal and do not 
affect the exterior brickwork without a Certificate of Appropriateness from the NYC 
Landmarks Preservation Commission.

ARTICLE 8: INSURANCE REQUIREMENTS 8.01 FedEx Self-Insurance: Landlord 
acknowledges that FedEx Ground Package System, Inc. may elect to self-insure some or all of its 
insurance obligations hereunder, provided that FedEx maintains a net worth of at least 
$5,000,000,000.00. 8.02 Liability Limits: If not self-insured, Tenant shall maintain Commercial 
General Liability insurance with limits of $15,000,000.00 per occurrence. 8.03 Cargo and ISP 
Liability: Tenant shall require all Independent Service Providers operating out of 74 Amity 
Street to maintain Automobile Liability insurance of no less than $2,000,000.00 combined 
single limit.

ARTICLE 9: ENVIRONMENTAL AND CLEANLINESS 9.01 Waste Management: Given 
the high volume of cardboard and packing materials, Tenant shall install an internal hydraulic 
baler on the ground floor. No waste or recycling materials shall be stored on the sidewalk of 
Amity Street. 9.02 Spill Prevention: Tenant shall provide "Spill Kits" and secondary 
containment for any hydraulic fluids used in the sorting machinery or elevator systems.

This third section addresses the intense logistical requirements of a FedEx Ground facility in a 
high-density, historic residential area. It focuses on the engineering of the loading systems and 
the strict management of the "last-mile" fleet.

COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT (FEDEX GROUND EDITION - PART 
3 OF 10)

ARTICLE 10: LOADING, LOGISTICS, AND DOCK ENGINEERING 10.01 Internal 
Loading Bay: Tenant is authorized to modify the ground floor of the 113-year-old shell to create 
a "Drive-In" loading zone. This zone must accommodate at least four (4) P-1000 step vans 
simultaneously. 10.02 High-Speed Roll-Up Doors: Tenant may install two (2) high-speed, 
insulated industrial roll-up doors. To maintain compliance with the Cobble Hill Historic 
District guidelines, these doors must be recessed at least five feet from the building line and 
finished in a "Bronze-Anodized" or "Historic Black" powder coat to minimize visual impact on 
Amity Street. 10.03 Scissor Lifts and Dock Levelers: Tenant shall install two (2) hydraulic pit-
mounted dock levelers (capacity 30,000 lbs) to bridge the gap between the varying heights of the 
independent contractor vehicles and the building’s primary sorting floor.

ARTICLE 11: TRAFFIC MITIGATION AND NEIGHBORHOOD RELATIONS 11.01 
Queuing Prohibition: Tenant acknowledges that Amity Street is a secondary residential street. 
Tenant shall not permit any FedEx-branded or ISP (Independent Service Provider) vehicles to 
queue, double-park, or idle on Amity Street or the surrounding blocks. 11.02 The "Staging 
Plan": All vehicles must be staged at a secondary off-site location (e.g., the FedEx Brooklyn 
Terminal) and dispatched to 74 Amity Street only when an internal loading slot is vacant. 11.03 
Noise Abatement: Tenant shall install "White Noise" backup alarms on all vehicles permanently 
stationed at this facility to minimize the "beep-beep" disturbance to residents of the adjacent 
townhomes during early morning (6:00 AM - 8:00 AM) dispatch.

ARTICLE 12: UTILITIES AND "SMART-POST" TECHNOLOGY 12.01 Data 
Infrastructure: Tenant shall install a dedicated 10-Gigabit fiber-optic backbone to support the 
real-time tracking and "Smart-Post" integration required for ground operations. 12.02 HVAC 
and Ventilation: Tenant shall install a high-efficiency rooftop HVAC unit (the "RTU"). Given 
the historic nature of the building, the RTU must be set back at least 15 feet from the facade to 
ensure it is not visible from the street level, as required by the NYC Landmarks Preservation 
Commission (LPC). 12.03 Electrical Sub-Metering: Tenant shall install a sub-meter for the 
"Sortation Spine" (Article 7.01) to monitor the high energy demand of the conveyor motors 
separately from general building lighting.

ARTICLE 13: PARKING AND SIDEWALK USE 13.01 Employee Parking: No parking is 
provided at the Premises. Tenant shall encourage employees and contractors to utilize public 
transportation (via the nearby Borough Hall subway hub). 13.02 Sidewalk Maintenance: In 
accordance with NYC Administrative Code, Tenant shall maintain the sidewalk in "Excellent" 
condition. This includes the repair of any cracks caused by the weight of FedEx step vans 
crossing the sidewalk into the internal loading bay. 13.03 Snow and Ice: Tenant shall utilize an 
automated sidewalk snow-melting system (hydronic or electric) or provide manual clearance 
within two (2) hours of any snowfall to ensure safe pedestrian passage on Amity Street.

ARTICLE 14: SIGNAGE AND BRANDING 14.01 The "Purple/Orange" Brand: Tenant 
may install one (1) non-illuminated "FedEx Ground" sign. The sign must be a "Blade" style or 
"Plaque" style, not exceeding 18 inches by 24 inches, to comply with the residential character of 
the LH-1 zoning. 14.02 Window Decals: Tenant may apply a frosted "FedEx" logo to the 
ground floor glass, provided it does not cover more than 10% of the total glass area.

ARTICLE 15: DEFAULT AND EVENTS OF DEFAULT 15.01 Events of Default: The 
occurrence of any one or more of the following shall constitute a material "Event of Default" by 
Tenant:

• 
Monetary Default: Failure to pay Base Rent or Additional Rent within five (5) business 
days after the due date.

• 
Covenant Default: Failure to perform any other term or condition (such as maintaining 
the historic facade or adhering to noise ordinances) within thirty (30) days after written 
notice.

• 
Interference with Neighbors: Repeated violation of the traffic and queuing prohibitions 
on Amity Street (Article 11.01) after two (2) written warnings in a twelve-month period.

• 
Insolvency: The appointment of a receiver for FedEx Ground Package System, Inc., or 
the filing of a petition for bankruptcy protection.

ARTICLE 16: REMEDIES UPON DEFAULT 16.01 Landlord’s Rights: Upon the 
occurrence of an Event of Default, Landlord may, at its option, exercise any of the following 
remedies:

• 
Termination: Terminate this Lease by providing five (5) days' notice, at which point 
Tenant shall immediately surrender the Premises.

pages_context.page_heading: COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT (FEDEX GROUND EDITION - PART
pages_context.page_text:
• 
Re-Entry: Re-enter the Premises, remove all FedEx sorting equipment and ISP vehicles, 
and change the locks without being liable for trespass.

• 
Damages: Recover from Tenant all unpaid Rent plus the "Worth at the Time of Award" 
of the amount by which the unpaid Rent for the remainder of the Term exceeds the 
amount of such rental loss that the Tenant proves could be reasonably avoided. 16.02 
Late Fees and Interest: Any sum not paid when due shall bear interest from the date due 
until paid at the "Default Rate" of twelve percent (12%) per annum or the maximum rate 
permitted by New York law.

ARTICLE 17: CORPORATE GUARANTEE AND FINANCIAL COVENANTS 17.01 
Corporate Guarantee: This Lease is backed by a full corporate guarantee from FedEx 
Corporation (the "Guarantor"). In the event of a default by FedEx Ground Package System, 
Inc., Landlord may proceed directly against the Guarantor without first exhausting remedies 
against the Tenant. 17.02 Financial Statements: Upon request, Tenant shall provide Landlord 
with a copy of its most recent Annual Report (Form 10-K) to verify continued financial viability.

ARTICLE 18: ASSIGNMENT AND SUBLETTING 18.01 Consent Required: Tenant shall 
not assign, mortgage, or encumber this Lease, nor sublet the Premises, without Landlord’s prior 
written consent, which shall not be unreasonably withheld. 18.02 Permitted Transfers: Tenant 
may assign this Lease or sublet the Premises to any subsidiary or affiliate of FedEx Corporation 
(e.g., FedEx Express or FedEx Freight) without Landlord’s consent, provided that the use 
remains a logistics-related operation and the Tenant provides ten (10) days' notice. 18.03 
Change of Control: Any transfer of more than fifty percent (50%) of the voting stock of Tenant 
shall be deemed an assignment requiring Landlord’s consent.

ARTICLE 19: LIENS 19.01 Mechanic’s Liens: Tenant shall not permit any mechanic’s, 
materialman’s, or other lien to be placed upon the Premises in connection with the "Purple Tail" 
build-out (Article 7). 19.02 Discharge of Lien: If any such lien is filed, Tenant shall cause the 
same to be discharged of record or "bonded over" within twenty (20) days after notice of the 
filing.

ARTICLE 20: INDEMNIFICATION 20.01 Mutual Indemnity: Tenant shall indemnify and 
hold Landlord harmless from any claims arising from Tenant’s operations, including accidents 
involving ISP vehicles. Landlord shall indemnify Tenant from claims arising from Landlord’s 
gross negligence or willful misconduct in the common areas (if any).

This fifth section focuses on the high-intensity safety and environmental requirements of a high-
volume FedEx Ground operation, specifically addressing the risks associated with automated 
machinery and large-scale parcel distribution.

COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT (FEDEX GROUND EDITION - PART 
5 OF 10)

ARTICLE 21: ENVIRONMENTAL COMPLIANCE AND HAZARDOUS MATERIALS 
21.01 Definition of Hazardous Materials: "Hazardous Materials" includes, but is not limited 
to, substances defined as such under CERCLA or RCRA, as well as petroleum, including crude

oil or any fraction thereof, and lithium-ion batteries. 21.02 FedEx Ground Operations: 
Landlord acknowledges that FedEx Ground transports third-party packages which may contain 
"Limited Quantity" hazardous materials (e.g., consumer commodities, aerosols, and small 
batteries). Tenant shall handle such materials in strict accordance with Department of 
Transportation (DOT) and Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration (PHMSA) 
regulations. 21.03 Spill Containment for Sorting Systems: Tenant shall install secondary 
containment systems under all hydraulic power units and grease-intensive components of the 
"Sortation Spine" (Article 7.01) to prevent any seepage into the concrete slab of the 74 Amity 
Street shell. 21.04 Prohibited Materials: Tenant shall not store bulk fuel, bulk lubricants, or 
perform vehicle maintenance (other than emergency minor repairs) within the Premises.

ARTICLE 22: FIRE AND LIFE SAFETY SYSTEMS 22.01 ESFR Sprinkler System: Due 
to the high-piled storage and automated conveyor configuration, Tenant shall install an Early 
Suppression, Fast Response (ESFR) sprinkler system throughout the building. This system 
must be designed to protect "Class IV" and "Plastic" commodities. 22.02 Fire Command 
Center: Tenant shall establish a Fire Command Center on the ground floor near the Amity Street 
entrance. This center will house the fire alarm control panel (FACP), graphic annunciator, and 
emergency communication system. 22.03 Smoke Heat Vents: Tenant is authorized to install 
automatic smoke and heat vents on the roof of the 113-year-old shell, provided they are low-
profile and pre-approved by the NYC Landmarks Preservation Commission (LPC).

ARTICLE 23: ACCESSIBILITY AND ADA COMPLIANCE 23.01 Facility Accessibility: 
Tenant shall be responsible for ensuring the building meets all Americans with Disabilities Act 
(ADA) and New York City Building Code (Chapter 11) requirements. 23.02 Employee Areas: 
All sorting mezzanines, administrative offices on the upper floors, and breakrooms must be 
accessible via a newly installed ADA-compliant elevator. 23.03 The Amity Street Interface: 
Tenant shall modify the sidewalk and building entrance to provide a flush, barrier-free entry for 
both personnel and equipment.

ARTICLE 24: SECURITY AND ACCESS CONTROL 24.01 Perimeter Security: Tenant 
shall maintain a "Secured Perimeter." Access to the sorting floor shall be restricted to badge-
holding employees and authorized Independent Service Providers (ISPs). 24.02 CCTV and 
Surveillance: Tenant shall install a minimum of 24 high-definition cameras covering all 
loading docks, the "Sortation Spine," and the exterior sidewalk. All footage must be retained for 
at least sixty (60) days. 24.03 Landlord Access: Landlord’s right of entry under Article 12 
(DHL Lease Reference) is specifically restricted here. Landlord must provide 48 hours' notice 
and may be denied entry to "Secure Sorting Zones" during peak "Package Sort" windows 
(typically 4:00 AM - 8:00 AM and 5:00 PM - 9:00 PM) to ensure operational safety.

ARTICLE 25: NOISE CONTROL AND VIBRATION 25.01 Acoustic Mitigation: Tenant 
shall install sound-dampening panels (NRC rating of 0.85 or higher) on the interior of the 
masonry walls shared with the adjacent residential townhomes. 25.02 Vibration Isolation: All 
conveyor motors and sorting divert-arms must be mounted on vibration-isolation pads to prevent 
structural resonance from affecting the 1900-era brick and timber construction of the 74 Amity 
Street shell. 25.03 Compliance with NYC Noise Code: Tenant’s total decibel output at the

pages_context.page_heading: COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT (FEDEX GROUND EDITION - PART
pages_context.page_text:
property line shall not exceed 55 dB(A) during nighttime hours (10:00 PM - 7:00 AM) and 65 
dB(A) during daytime hours.

This sixth section establishes the financial and legal hierarchy between the Lease and any 
institutional financing. Given FedEx’s corporate strength, these clauses are tailored to ensure that 
their multi-million dollar "Sortation Spine" investment is protected from any potential Landlord 
insolvency.

COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT (FEDEX GROUND EDITION - PART 
6 OF 10)

ARTICLE 26: SUBORDINATION, NON-DISTURBANCE, AND ATTORNMENT 
(SNDA) 26.01 Subordination: This Lease, and all rights of Tenant hereunder, are and shall be 
subject and subordinate to any ground lease, mortgage, or deed of trust (each, a "Mortgage") now 
or hereafter existing against the Premises. 26.02 Non-Disturbance Requirement: Landlord’s 
right to subordinate this Lease to a future Mortgage is expressly contingent upon Landlord 
obtaining a Non-Disturbance Agreement from the Mortgagee. Such agreement must state that 
so long as FedEx Ground is not in material default, its possession of 74 Amity Street, its sorting 
operations, and its leasehold improvements shall not be disturbed, even in the event of a 
foreclosure against the Landlord. 26.03 Attornment: If any Mortgagee succeeds to the interest 
of the Landlord, Tenant shall "attorn" to such successor as its new Landlord. Tenant shall 
recognize such successor as the owner of 74 Amity Street and continue to perform all lease 
obligations, provided the successor assumes all of Landlord’s duties.

ARTICLE 27: TRANSFER OF LANDLORD’S INTEREST 27.01 Right to Sell: Landlord 
has the unrestricted right to sell or transfer the Premises. However, Landlord must provide 
Tenant with at least thirty (30) days' prior written notice of any sale to a third party. 27.02 
Release of Liability: Upon a sale and the assumption of this Lease by the purchaser, the original 
Landlord (Downstate at LICH Holding Company, Inc.) shall be released from all future liability 
under the Lease. 27.03 Transfer of Security: In the event of a sale, the Letter of Credit (Article 
3.03) or any cash security shall be transferred to the new owner. Tenant agrees to cooperate with 
the issuing bank to amend the "Beneficiary" name on the Letter of Credit to reflect the new 
Landlord.

ARTICLE 28: ESTOPPEL CERTIFICATES 28.01 Tenant’s Statement: Within fifteen (15) 
business days of a request by Landlord or a prospective lender, Tenant shall execute an Estoppel 
Certificate certifying the current status of the Lease (e.g., Rent paid, expiration date, absence of 
defaults). 28.02 FedEx Corporate Form: Landlord acknowledges that FedEx Ground typically 
utilizes its own standard corporate Estoppel form. Landlord agrees to accept such form provided 
it contains the commercially reasonable certifications required by Landlord’s lender.

ARTICLE 29: MORTGAGEE PROTECTION AND CURE RIGHTS 29.01 Notice to 
Mortgagee: If Landlord defaults on any of its obligations (such as failing to provide a required 
structural sign-off for the facade), Tenant shall notify Landlord’s Mortgagee simultaneously with 
the notice to Landlord. 29.02 Mortgagee’s Right to Cure: Any Mortgagee shall have the right, 
but not the obligation, to cure a Landlord default within thirty (30) days after receipt of notice.

Tenant shall not terminate the Lease for a Landlord default unless the Mortgagee has been given 
this opportunity to cure.

ARTICLE 30: ATTORNMENT TO GROUND LESSOR 30.01 Ground Lease Status: 
Landlord represents that it owns the Premises in "Fee Simple" and that there is currently no 
ground lease. In the event a ground lease is created, Tenant agrees to attorn to the ground lessor 
under the same terms as Article 26.03.

ARTICLE 31: QUIET ENJOYMENT 31.01 Uninterrupted Possession: Landlord covenants 
that so long as Tenant pays the Rent and performs its covenants, Tenant shall peaceably and 
quietly have, hold, and enjoy the Premises throughout the Term without hindrance from 
Landlord or any person claiming through Landlord. Landlord specifically acknowledges that 
"Quiet Enjoyment" includes the right to operate the FedEx sorting facility 24 hours a day, 7 days 
a week, 365 days a year.

ARTICLE 32: SURRENDER OF PREMISES 32.01 Condition Upon Surrender: Upon the 
expiration of the Term or earlier termination, Tenant shall surrender the Premises to Landlord in 
"Broom Clean" condition and in good order and repair, reasonable wear and tear and damage by 
casualty excepted. 32.02 Decommissioning the Sortation Spine: Tenant shall, at its sole cost, 
remove all proprietary FedEx equipment, including:

• 
All multi-tier automated conveyor systems, motors, and divert-arms.

• 
All FedEx-branded electronic signage, scanners, and "Dimensioner" towers.

• 
All internal steel mezzanines and catwalks (unless Landlord requests in writing that they 
remain for a future industrial tenant). 32.03 Restoration of Structural Elements: Any 
structural reinforcements made to the floors or walls of the 74 Amity Street shell (as 
described in Article 7) shall remain the property of the Landlord and shall not be 
removed, as they have become "fixtures" to the real property. 32.04 Facade Reversion: 
If Tenant modified the exterior masonry to accommodate the drive-in loading bay, 
Landlord may require Tenant to restore the facade to its original 1900-era "Shell" 
configuration, utilizing salvaged brick and mortar that matches the Cobble Hill Historic 
District aesthetic.

ARTICLE 33: "DE-BRANDING" AND LOGO REMOVAL 33.01 Removal of Marks: 
Upon vacating the Premises, Tenant shall remove all "FedEx Ground" and "Purple/Orange" 
branding. This includes the removal of the exterior blade sign and any frosted window decals. 
33.02 Patching and Repair: Any holes or damage to the historic brickwork caused by the 
mounting of signs or security cameras must be professionally patched and tinted to match the 
surrounding masonry.

ARTICLE 34: HOLDING OVER 34.01 Status of Holdover: If Tenant remains in possession 
after the expiration of the Term without a written extension, Tenant shall be a "tenant-at-
sufferance." 34.02 Liquidated Damages for Holdover: During the first sixty (60) days of any 
holdover, Tenant shall pay Base Rent at 150% of the rate in effect during the final month of the 
Term. If the holdover exceeds sixty days, the rate shall increase to 200%. 34.03 Consequential 
Damages: Tenant acknowledges that Landlord may be negotiating with other high-value

pages_context.page_heading: COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT (FEDEX GROUND EDITION - PART
pages_context.page_text:
logistics providers. If Tenant’s failure to surrender the Premises causes Landlord to lose a new 
tenant, FedEx Ground shall be liable for all of Landlord’s consequential damages.

ARTICLE 35: ABANDONMENT 35.01 Presumption of Abandonment: If Tenant vacates 
the Premises for thirty (30) consecutive days while in default of Rent, the Premises shall be 
deemed abandoned. 35.02 Disposal of Property: Landlord may dispose of any "FedEx" 
proprietary equipment left behind during abandonment. Tenant shall be liable for all costs 
associated with the removal and disposal of such high-density steel sorting equipment.

ARTICLE 36: END-OF-TERM ENVIRONMENTAL AUDIT 36.01 Exit Assessment: 
Within thirty (30) days prior to the expiration date, Tenant shall commission a "Phase I 
Environmental Site Assessment" (ESA). 36.02 Remediation: If the ESA reveals any 
contamination (e.g., hydraulic fluid leaks or battery acid seepage) caused by FedEx operations or 
its Independent Service Providers, Tenant shall perform all necessary remediation at its sole cost 
before the Lease is officially terminated.

This eighth section focuses on the legal "mechanics" of the agreement, ensuring that FedEx 
Ground—a massive global entity—is protected against local New York City disruptions and that 
the communication channels between the parties are ironclad.

COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT (FEDEX GROUND EDITION - PART 
8 OF 10)

ARTICLE 37: FORCE MAJEURE AND OPERATIONAL INTERRUPTION 37.01 
Definition: "Force Majeure" shall include, but is not limited to: acts of God, war, civil 
commotion, strikes, lockouts, or other labor disputes; inability to obtain materials or energy; fire, 
flood, or other casualty; and governmental regulations or controls (specifically including 
emergency closures of the Brooklyn-Battery Tunnel or BQE by the NYCDOT). 37.02 Effect on 
Performance: If either party is delayed or prevented from performing any non-monetary 
obligation by reason of Force Majeure, the period of such delay shall be excused and the time for 
performance extended. 37.03 Last-Mile Exception: If a Force Majeure event (e.g., a city-wide 
transit strike or bridge closure) prevents FedEx from accessing the Premises with its delivery 
fleet for more than five (5) consecutive business days, Base Rent shall be abated by fifty 
percent (50%) for the duration of the access restriction.

ARTICLE 38: NOTICES AND COMMUNICATIONS 38.01 Official Notices: All notices 
shall be in writing and delivered via (a) Certified Mail, (b) a nationally recognized overnight 
courier (FedEx Priority Overnight shall be the preferred and deemed method), or (c) electronic 
mail with a follow-up hard copy. 38.02 Addresses:

• 
To Landlord: Downstate at LICH Holding Company, Inc., Attn: Asset Management, 
1290 Avenue of the Americas, New York, NY 10104.

• 
To Tenant: FedEx Ground Package System, Inc., Attn: Managing Director of Real 
Estate, 1000 FedEx Drive, Moon Township, PA 15108.

• 
Emergency Site Contact: FedEx Facility Manager, 74 Amity Street, Brooklyn, NY 
11201.

ARTICLE 39: GOVERNING LAW AND VENUE 39.01 Jurisdiction: This Lease shall be 
governed by the laws of the State of New York. 39.02 Venue Selection: Any litigation arising 
out of this Lease shall be filed in the Kings County Supreme Court. Both parties waive any right 
to remove such action to Federal Court unless diversity of citizenship is the sole basis for 
jurisdiction and the amount in controversy exceeds $5,000,000.00.

ARTICLE 40: WAIVER OF JURY TRIAL 40.01 Mutual Waiver: TO THE MAXIMUM 
EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, LANDLORD AND TENANT EACH HEREBY AGREE 
NOT TO ELECT A TRIAL BY JURY OF ANY ISSUE TRIABLE OF RIGHT BY JURY, AND 
WAIVE ANY RIGHT TO TRIAL BY JURY FULLY TO THE EXTENT THAT ANY SUCH 
RIGHT SHALL NOW OR FOREVER EXIST WITH REGARD TO THIS LEASE.

ARTICLE 41: BROKERAGE COMMISSIONS 41.01 Designated Broker: The parties 
acknowledge that Cushman & Wakefield, Inc. represented the Landlord and CBRE, Inc. 
represented the Tenant in this transaction. 41.02 Payment Responsibility: Landlord shall pay all 
brokerage commissions per separate agreement. Each party shall indemnify the other against any 
claims for "Finder's Fees" by any other person or entity.

ARTICLE 42: RELOCATION AND EXPANSION RIGHTS 42.01 No Relocation: Landlord 
shall have no right to relocate Tenant to any other space during the Term. 42.02 Right of First 
Refusal (ROFR): In the event Landlord intends to sell the Fee Interest in 74 Amity Street or any 
adjacent property (e.g., Block 295, Lot 8), Tenant shall have a Right of First Refusal to 
purchase said property on the same terms and conditions as any bona fide third-party offer.

ARTICLE 43: CONFIDENTIALITY 43.01 Non-Disclosure: Landlord agrees to keep the 
specific design of the "Sortation Spine" and FedEx’s internal security protocols confidential and 
shall not disclose them to any third party, including competitors like DHL or UPS, without prior 
written consent.

ARTICLE 44: EXHIBIT A - FEDEX GROUND INFRASTRUCTURE SPECIFICATIONS 
44.01 Structural Loading and Floor Reinforcement:

• 
Ground Floor: The existing slab shall be excavated and replaced with a 10-inch 
reinforced concrete slab (4,000 PSI) capable of supporting a static load of 600 lbs/sq. 
ft. to accommodate the impact of heavy P-1000 step vans.

• 
Upper Floors: Tenant shall install a steel "super-structure" internally to support the 
Sortation Spine. This structure must be independent of the historic brick party walls to 
avoid structural stress on adjacent properties.

44.02 Conveyor and Sortation Engineering:

• 
The Spine: A multi-level, high-speed cross-belt sorter shall be installed. The system 
must include automated "Divert-Arms" capable of processing 4,500 packages per hour.

• 
Vertical Incline Conveyors: To transport parcels between the four floors, Tenant shall 
install spiral conveyors with a minimum diameter of 8 feet, ensuring smooth transition of 
oversized "FedEx Ground" packages.

pages_context.page_heading: ARTICLE 45: EXHIBIT B - ELECTRICAL, DATA, AND ENERGY MANAGEMENT
pages_context.page_text:
ARTICLE 45: EXHIBIT B - ELECTRICAL, DATA, AND ENERGY MANAGEMENT 
45.01 Primary Service: Tenant is authorized to install a new high-voltage service riser from the 
street. The main switchgear shall be rated for 1,600 Amps, 480/277 Volts, 3-Phase. 45.02 The 
"Smart-Hub" Data Suite:

• 
A dedicated MDF (Main Distribution Frame) room shall be constructed on the 3rd floor.

• 
The room must be equipped with redundant cooling (N+1) and a 100kW UPS 
(Uninterruptible Power Supply) system to ensure the sorting facility remains 
operational during local grid fluctuations.

45.03 Lighting: All lighting shall be high-output LED fixtures with motion sensors to comply 
with NYCECC (New York City Energy Conservation Code).

ARTICLE 46: EXHIBIT C - EXTERIOR HISTORIC PRESERVATION AND 
APERTURES 46.01 Masonry Restoration: Tenant shall perform a full "Chemical Clean" of 
the front facade using non-abrasive methods approved by the Landmarks Preservation 
Commission (LPC). 46.02 The Loading Aperture: The ground floor "Carriage" entrance shall 
be widened to exactly 14 feet. The lintel must be replaced with a steel beam clad in cast-iron 
plates to match the industrial heritage of the neighborhood. 46.03 Signage Plaque: Tenant shall 
install a "Bronze Plaque" style sign. The plaque must be chemically aged to appear historic and 
must be mounted directly into the mortar joints (not the brick face) to preserve the 113-year-old 
masonry.

ARTICLE 47: EXHIBIT D - NOISE, VIBRATION, AND EMISSION SHIELDING 47.01 
Acoustic Shell: The entire first floor (loading zone) shall be lined with K-13 spray-applied 
acoustic insulation to prevent the sound of van engines and sorting motors from echoing. 47.02 
Vibration Dampening: All heavy-duty sorting motors shall be mounted on "Spring-and-
Rubber" isolators. Tenant shall conduct a vibration study post-installation to ensure no "Seismic 
Transfer" is felt in the adjoining residential townhomes.

ARTICLE 48: EXHIBIT E - PARCEL CHUTE AND EMERGENCY EGRESS 48.01 
Emergency Chutes: Tenant shall install two (2) emergency parcel-clearance chutes that allow 
for the rapid evacuation of the upper floors in the event of a fire or system failure. 48.02 Life 
Safety Integration: All automated conveyors must be integrated with the Fire Alarm Control 
Panel (FACP) to ensure an Automatic Emergency Stop (E-Stop) occurs the moment smoke or 
heat is detected.

ARTICLE 49: FINAL EXECUTION AND BINDING EFFECT 49.01 Full Execution: This 
Lease shall not be binding upon either party until it has been executed and delivered by both 
Landlord and Tenant. 49.02 Corporate Authority: Each individual executing this Lease on 
behalf of a corporation represents and warrants that he or she is duly authorized to execute and 
deliver this Lease on behalf of said corporation. 49.03 Successors and Assigns: The terms and 
conditions of this Lease shall inure to the benefit of and be binding upon the respective 
successors and permitted assigns of the parties hereto.

ARTICLE 50: SURVIVAL 50.01 Post-Term Obligations: Any liability of Tenant for the 
payment of Rent, and any obligation to perform an act or to indemnify or hold harmless 
Landlord, which arose during the Term of this Lease, shall survive the expiration or earlier 
termination of this Lease.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have caused this Lease to be executed by their duly 
authorized officers on the date first written above.

LANDLORD:

Downstate at LICH Holding Company, Inc.
